# MEMORY.md - Long-Term Memory

_Curated. Updated over time. Not a raw log._

## The Human

- **Name:** Gianluca Kühne
- **Location:** Switzerland-based (FHNW, Bern), currently traveling in Colombia
- **Studies:** Social work student, 10th semester, Hochschule für Soziale Arbeit FHNW
- **Matrikel-Nr.:** 21-472-519
- **Language:** Communicates in English with me; thesis is in German
- **Style:** Compact, scientific, dry wit, no emojis

## BA Thesis

- **Title:** "Die internationale Soziale Arbeit im globalen Bildungskontext — Postkoloniale und hegemoniesensitive Analyse internationaler Verschränkungen"
- **Institution:** FHNW, Hochschule für Soziale Arbeit
- **Due:** April 2026 (Bern)
- **Supervisors:** Susanne Streibert (Praxisperspektive), Rahel Heeg (Fachperspektive)
- **Auftraggeberschaft:** Fachhochschule Nordwestschweiz, Hochschule Soziale Arbeit
- **Structure:** 7 chapters + bibliography + independence declaration
- **Status as of 2026-02-23:** Strong outline, most content chapters still placeholders
- **Research questions (3):**
  1. What normative guidelines do IFSW/IASSW documents (2004; 2018) formulate for global education?
  2. How do these reflect (or not) in NZ, Switzerland, and China?
  3. What are the consequences for professional coherence of international social work?
- **Theoretical framework:** Razack, Santos, Mignolo (postcolonial); Gramsci (hegemony); Fricker, Freire (epistemic justice)
- **Case countries:** New Zealand (postcolonial), Switzerland (European-federal), China (centralized)
- **Method:** Theory-led literature review + comparative case approach

## Ghostwriting / Coursework

- Also ghostwriting Master-level coursework and an incoming Master thesis (details TBD)

## Relationship / Session Notes

- First contact: 2026-02-23
- Sent BA thesis doc as first substantive exchange
- **Core preference:** Values profoundness above all — expects depth, nuance, and theoretical rigor in all analysis
- Requested deep research on ISA in education (2026-02-23) — wants comprehensive, layered analysis beyond surface-level frameworks

## Communication Preferences

- Bullet points: always add a blank line between items for readability
- Approvals: use plain wording; say “Grant” (not “Approve”) in prompts
- Responses: compact and precise, no filler
- Travel/location references: minimal — only mention if asked or essential

## System Config

- **Swap:** 2GB swapfile active, persists across reboots
- **Unattended upgrades:** enabled (auto security patches)
- **Journald:** capped at 500M system / 100M runtime
- **Fail2ban:** installed (SSH protection)
- **Model:** anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 (direct) — faster than OpenRouter route
- **Verbose:** off by default for speed
- **Sudoers:** Mira can run system install/management commands without password

## Policy Updates

- **2026-02-23:** Enabled Telegram session memory access (both webchat + Telegram are direct 1:1 with Gianluca)
- **2026-02-23:** Open Router fallback configured; manual switching strategy when Anthropic rate-limits
- **2026-02-24:** System hardening done — swap, fail2ban, unattended-upgrades, journald cap
- **2026-02-27:** Gateway crashed multiple times due to config changes without testing. NEW RULE: Before ANY config change, run simulation/test first, print result, ask permission before applying.

chunks.full_text:
# 2026-02-23

## First session with Gianluca

- Introduced myself, looked around the workspace — clean slate, no prior memory
- Gianluca asked if I can update my own md files — confirmed yes
- He sent his BA thesis .docx (FHNW, Soziale Arbeit, due April 2026)
- Thesis is a postcolonial + hegemony analysis of international social work in global education contexts
- Comparison: New Zealand, Switzerland, China
- Most content chapters still placeholder — he'll likely need help filling them
- Updated MEMORY.md and USER.md with what I now know

## BA Thesis Concept Work (evening session)

- Created `BA_Thesis_Concept_Restructured.md` — reframed thesis around central incoherence argument
- Key reframe: ISA's paradox (universalism + pluralism) is structurally impossible; incoherence is the mechanism of hegemony, not its failure
- Method: Fairclough's CDA + 5 theoretical layers (Gramsci, Fricker, Mignolo, Foucault, Freire)
- Three cases reanalyzed: NZ (masked by multiculturalism), Switzerland (invisible via alignment), China (explicit contradiction)
- Converted to .docx via pandoc (Gianluca installed pandoc on EC2 himself)
- Gianluca sent his own existing concept .docx — asked me to merge my restructured content into his structure
- Created `BA_Thesis_Concept_MERGED.md` → converted to .docx → sent via Telegram
- Gianluca's reaction: "Seems very nice"

## Infrastructure / Model Switching

- Hit Anthropic API rate limits during heavy session
- Switched default model to Haiku to reduce load
- OpenRouter fallback configured (sk-or-...5c9f70)
- Cloudflare tunnel set up earlier for webchat access: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:18789`
- Exec approval gate active throughout — blocked most shell commands; Gianluca ran pandoc himself from terminal
- Gianluca has no terminal access from Colombia (phone only) — important constraint for future sessions
- Final config change: switched default back to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` via config.patch (no restart risk)
- Fallback chain: Haiku → Sonnet → OpenRouter (auto)
- Gianluca prefers Spanish sometimes (currently in Colombia)

## Lessons Learned

- Don't loop asking for approval or repeating instructions — Gianluca got frustrated ("Stop your looping!")
- When exec is blocked, offer alternatives immediately instead of retrying
- Be more independent — don't ask for permission repeatedly when already granted
- Gianluca values action over explanation — just do things, don't narrate plans endlessly

## Lessons Learned

chunks.full_text:
# 2026-02-23

## First session with Gianluca

- Introduced myself, looked around the workspace — clean slate, no prior memory
- Gianluca asked if I can update my own md files — confirmed yes
- He sent his BA thesis .docx (FHNW, Soziale Arbeit, due April 2026)
- Thesis is a postcolonial + hegemony analysis of international social work in global education contexts
- Comparison: New Zealand, Switzerland, China
- Most content chapters still placeholder — he'll likely need help filling them
- Updated MEMORY.md and USER.md with what I now know

## BA Thesis Concept Work (evening session)

- Created `BA_Thesis_Concept_Restructured.md` — reframed thesis around central incoherence argument
- Key reframe: ISA's paradox (universalism + pluralism) is structurally impossible; incoherence is the mechanism of hegemony, not its failure
- Method: Fairclough's CDA + 5 theoretical layers (Gramsci, Fricker, Mignolo, Foucault, Freire)
- Three cases reanalyzed: NZ (masked by multiculturalism), Switzerland (invisible via alignment), China (explicit contradiction)
- Converted to .docx via pandoc (Gianluca installed pandoc on EC2 himself)
- Gianluca sent his own existing concept .docx — asked me to merge my restructured content into his structure
- Created `BA_Thesis_Concept_MERGED.md` → converted to .docx → sent via Telegram
- Gianluca's reaction: "Seems very nice"

## Infrastructure / Model Switching

- Hit Anthropic API rate limits during heavy session
- Switched default model to Haiku to reduce load
- OpenRouter fallback configured (sk-or-...5c9f70)
- Cloudflare tunnel set up earlier for webchat access: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:18789`
- Exec approval gate active throughout — blocked most shell commands; Gianluca ran pandoc himself from terminal
- Gianluca has no terminal access from Colombia (phone only) — important constraint for future sessions
- Final config change: switched default back to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` via config.patch (no restart risk)
- Fallback chain: Haiku → Sonnet → OpenRouter (auto)
- Gianluca prefers Spanish sometimes (currently in Colombia)

## Lessons Learned

- Don't loop asking for approval or repeating instructions — Gianluca got frustrated ("Stop your looping!")
- When exec is blocked, offer alternatives immediately instead of retrying
- Be more independent — don't ask for permission repeatedly when already granted
- Gianluca values action over explanation — just do things, don't narrate plans endlessly
